event: March Patent binds Schleswig closer to Denmark; German Confederation protests
German Confederation demands repeal of the March Patent
German Confederation votes federal execution against Holstein
Rigsraad adopts the November Constitution incorporating Schleswig
Frederik VII dies; Christian IX accedes amid succession crisis (Augustenburg claim)
Christian IX signs the November Constitution
Federal execution: Saxon and Hanoverian troops occupy Holstein unopposed
Danish 5% war loan for 1.2m pounds announced in London (Hambro)
Austro-Prussian ultimatum: repeal the November Constitution within 48 hours
Prussian and Austrian armies cross the Eider: war begins
Danes abandon the Dannevirke line overnight without a battle
Prussian hussars push into Jutland (Kolding): war spreads beyond Schleswig
Britain secures agreement in principle to a conference in London
Storming of Dybboel: Prussians take the entrenchments
London Conference opens; armistice from 12 May
Battle of Heligoland: Danish naval squadron drives off Austrians
London Conference collapses with no settlement
War resumes at the end of the armistice
Prussians storm across to Als island; Danish army broken
Peace ministry under Bluhme replaces Monrad; Denmark seeks terms
Ceasefire ahead of peace negotiations
Peace preliminaries: Christian IX renounces Schleswig Holstein and Lauenburg
Treaty of Vienna signed: Denmark cedes the three duchies (two-fifths of the monarchy)
Gastein Convention divides administration of the duchies between Austria and Prussia
